#  Add Columns to a Budget View for ERP Job Cost Transaction Detail 
##### ERP DOCUMENTATION
**Each Procore-built ERP integration offers its own unique feature set.** Your integration might not support the action described on this page.
To learn about your integration and its supported features, see [Which ERP integrations are supported by Procore?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-which-erp-integrations-are-supported-by-procore)
#####  Important
The steps below are designed to be used by Procore customers integrated with supported ERP systems who want to import job cost transaction data created in their ERP system into Procore.
##  Background 
A Budget View defines the column layout in the Budget tool. Procore includes four default views, which you can use as is, customize, or supplement by creating additional views.. For instructions, see [Set up a New Budget View](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/admin-company/tutorials/set-up-a-new-budget-view).
If you plan to use ERP Integrations' job cost transaction syncing, you want to add the recommended columns to relevant budget view(s) and complete the following steps.
#####  Notes
  * These steps show how to modify the standard **Procore ERP Budget view** , but they can be applied to any other standard or custom budget view. 
  * If you have previously completed the steps in [Add the 'ERP Direct Costs' Column to a Procore Budget View for ERP Integrations](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/admin-company/tutorials/add-the-erp-direct-costs-column-to-a-procore-budget-view-for-erp-integrations), you will want to remove both '**ERP Direct Costs** ' and the **'ERP Job to Date Costs'** source column from the target budget views before starting these steps. 


##  Things to Consider 
  * **Required User Permissions** :
    * 'Admin' on the Company Admin tool.  
AND
    * Use the **'View Direct Cost Details'** granular permission in your templates to control which users can click a value in the **'Direct Costs'** column to view job cost transaction details in the Budget tool. See [Grant Granular Permissions in a Permission Template](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/permissions-company/tutorials/grant-granular-permissions-in-a-project-permissions-template).
  * **Requirements** :
    * Enable these Project Tools:
      * Budget Tool
      * Direct Costs Tool
    * Enable job cost transaction syncing on your project. See [Enable ERP Job Cost Transaction Syncing on a Procore Project.](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/erp-integrations-company/tutorials/enable-erp-job-cost-transaction-syncing-on-a-procore-project)
    * Set up the desired budget view(s) for your ERP-integrated project. See [Set up a New Budget View](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/admin-company/tutorials/set-up-a-new-budget-view) and [Which budget views should I add to my projects?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-which-budget-views-should-i-add-to-my-projects)


##  Steps 
  1. Navigate to the Company **Admin** tool.
  2. Under **Tool Settings** , click **Budget**.
  3. Locate the budget view that you want to modify. Then click that budget view in the list. In this example, we will modify the Procore ERP Budget view.
  4. Under **Column Configuration** , click **Configure Columns**.  
This opens the 'Configure Columns' window. Continue with the next steps.


### Step 1: Add an 'ERP JTD Cost Details' Source Column
The first step is to create an 'ERP Job to Date (JTD) Cost Details' source column.
  1. In the 'Configure Columns' window, click **Create Source Column**.
  2. Under **New Source Column** , do the following:
    1. **Column Name**. Enter a name for the new column name.  
For example, type: `ERP JTD Cost Details`
    2. **Column Source**. Select _Direct Costs_ at the source category from the drop-down list.
    3. **Direct Costs**. Place a checkmark in this box. Then, in the boxes that appear:
       * **Type**. Place a checkmark in the _Invoice_ , _Expense_ , _Payroll,_ and _Subcontractor Invoices_ boxes.   
_Note_ : The Subcontractor Invoices checkbox is only visible when:
         * Your Procore Administrator has enabled the job cost transaction syncing feature for ERP Integrations.
         * Job cost transaction syncing is enabled on one (1) project in your company account. See [Enable ERP Job Cost Transaction Syncing on a Procore Project](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/erp-integrations-company/tutorials/enable-erp-job-cost-transaction-syncing-on-a-procore-project).
       * **Status**. Place a checkmark in one or more boxes to limit the details to items in the selected status. In this example, we have selected _Approved_. However, your choices include: _Draft_ , _Pending_ , _Revise and Resubmit,_ and/or _Approved_.   
  
![Step 1](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/2725411f0a67856560b0f0d75e495477/03e4093ef867f39be238eaa56d8486f4/step-1.jpeg?fm=webp)  

  3. Choose from these options:
     * If this is a new column, click **Create**.
     * If this is an existing column but you want to save the new settings to a new column, click **Save as New**.
     * If this is an existing column and you want to save the new settings, click **Update**.


### Step 2: Add a 'Direct Costs' Source Column
The next step is to create a 'Direct Costs' source column.
  1. In the 'Configure Columns' window, click **Create Source Column**.
  2. Under **New Source Column** , do the following:
    1. **Column Name**. Enter a name for the new column name.  
For example, type: `Direct Costs`
    2. **Column Source**. Select _Direct Costs_ at the source category from the drop-down list.
    3. **Direct Costs**. Place a checkmark in this box. Then, in the boxes that appear:
       * **Type**. Place a checkmark in the _Invoice_ , _Expense_ , and _Payroll_ boxes.   
_Note_ : Always ensure that you remove the checkmark in the _Subcontractor Invoices_ box.
       * **Status**. Place a checkmark in one or more boxes to limit the details to items in the selected status. In this example, we have selected _Approved_. However, your choices include: _Draft_ , _Pending_ , _Revise and Resubmit,_ and/or _Approved_.   
  
![Step 2](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/b0e254cd5d7a4d5d65ac41000dd60b4a/d3912204fa6b43406fbfc20272b5bd39/step-2.jpeg?fm=webp)  

  3. Choose from these options:
     * If this is a new column, click **Create**.
     * If this is an existing column and you want to save the new settings, click **Update**.
     * If this is an existing column but you want to save the new settings to a new column, click **Save as New**.


### Step 3: Add a 'Commitment Invoices' Source Column
The final step is to create a 'Commitment Invoices' source column.
#####  Notes
  * This column does NOT include data from the subcontractor invoices created in Procore.
  * See [Which ERP integrations are supported by Procore?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-which-erp-integrations-are-supported-by-procore) for details about how your ERP system syncs Direct Costs with Procore.


  1. In the 'Configure Columns' window, click **Create Source Column**.
  2. Under **New Source Column** , do the following:
    1. **Column Name**. Enter a name for the new column name.  
For example, type: `Sub`
    2. **Column Source**. Select _Subcontractor Invoices_ at the source category from the drop-down list.
    3. **Direct Costs**. Place a checkmark in this box. Then, in the boxes that appear:
       * **Type**. Place a checkmark in the _Subcontractor Invoices_ box.
       * **Income Options**. Choose one of the options buttons for _Gross_ or _Net (Gross less Retainage)_. In this example, we kept the default setting of _Gross_. However, you can choose the best option for your project's budget.
       * **Status**. Place a checkmark in one or more boxes to limit the details to items in the selected status. In this example, we have selected _Approved_. However, your choices include: _Draft_ , _Pending_ , _Revise and Resubmit,_ and/or _Approved_.   
  
![Erp Subcontractor Invoices](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/965337057bb7a530e93426f9e207ab4b/e5db33d04290c7a7401f46182f3d55df/erp-subcontractor-invoices.png?fm=webp)  

  3. Choose from these options:
     * If this is a new column, click **Create**.
     * If this is an existing column and you want to save the new settings, click **Update**.
     * If this is an existing column but you want to save the new settings to a new column, click **Save as New**.


## See Also
  * [Sync Job Cost Transactions from an Integrated ERP into Procore](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/erp-integrations-company/tutorials/sync-job-cost-transactions-from-an-integrated-erp-into-procore)
  * [Enable ERP Job Cost Transaction Syncing on a Procore Project](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/erp-integrations-company/tutorials/enable-erp-job-cost-transaction-syncing-on-a-procore-project)
  * [Enable or Disable ERP Direct Exports](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/erp-integrations-company/tutorials/enable-or-disable-erp-direct-exports)
title: Add Columns to a Budget View for ERP Job Cost Transaction Detail
description: How to update the Procore ERP Budget view with the recommended source columns for the ERP job cost transaction details syncing feature.
